commonMedications.name: Semaglutide / GLP-1 (Ozempic/Wegovy) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D covers for diabetes (tier 3). $25 copay typical. Without coverage ~$900/mo. IRA negotiated price TBD.

commonMedications.name: Levothyroxine (Synthroid) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Atorvastatin 20mg [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Montelukast (Singulair) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Hydrochlorothiazide (HCTZ) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Olmesartan/Medoxomil (Benicar) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1-2; $4-15 copay

commonMedications.name: Albuterol inhaler [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D tier 2; $10-25 copay. IRA cap applies.

commonMedications.name: Metformin 500mg [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Allopurinol (gout) [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Lisinopril [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Topiramate (Topamax) [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1-2; $4-15 copay

commonMedications.name: Metoprolol (Lopressor) [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Atorvastatin 20mg [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

medicine.pharmacyAccess: CVS, Walgreens, Walmart, Kroger pharmacies widely available. $4 generic programs at Walmart/Kroger. Mail-order via OptumRx, Express Scripts, or Mark Cuban Cost Plus Drugs.
medicine.notes: US prices shown with Medicare Part D coverage. Without insurance, use GoodRx or Cost Plus Drugs for significant savings on generics. IRA Inflation Reduction Act caps insulin at $35/mo and out-of-pocket at $2,000/yr starting 2025.

categories.name: Dining Out
categories.seniorDiscounts: Many local restaurants offer early bird specials. Senior discounts at some chains (10-15%).
categories.notes: Lunch for 2 at moderate restaurant ~$30-35 including tax (5.3% meals tax) and 18% tip, 2x/week = ~$120/mo. Norfolk has excellent local dining in Ghent and downtown.

categories.name: Cable + 1 Gig Internet
categories.seniorDiscounts: ACP broadband subsidy ended 2024. Cox offers occasional promo rates for new customers.
categories.notes: Cox Gigablast ~$100/mo (most available). Verizon Fios Gigabit $90/mo where available (28% of city). Both include basic cable/streaming options.

categories.name: Amazon Prime
categories.seniorDiscounts: No senior discount. EBT/Medicaid holders qualify for Prime Access at $6.99/mo.
categories.notes: Includes Prime Video, free delivery. $14.99/mo or $139/yr.

categories.name: Gym/Fitness
categories.seniorDiscounts: Norfolk recreation centers offer senior rates. SilverSneakers included with many Medicare Advantage plans.
categories.notes: Planet Fitness $10-25/mo. City recreation centers ~$30/mo. YMCA of South Hampton Roads ~$55/mo. SilverSneakers (free with Medicare Advantage) accepted at many locations.

categories.name: Parks/Outdoors
categories.seniorDiscounts: Norfolk Botanical Garden offers senior admission rates. Many parks and beaches are free.
categories.notes: Norfolk Botanical Garden ($15 admission, annual pass $60). Ocean View Beach free. Town Point Park free. Norfolk has 100+ parks. Mostly free outdoor recreation.

categories.name: Books/Library
categories.seniorDiscounts: Norfolk Public Library is free with proof of residency.
categories.notes: Norfolk Public Library — 10 branches including Slover Library downtown. Free e-books, audiobooks via Libby. Free WiFi and computer access.

categories.name: Coffee/Cafes
categories.seniorDiscounts: No specific senior discounts at most cafes.
categories.notes: Ghent has excellent independent cafes (Cure Coffeehouse, Equinox). Coffee ~$4.50-6 for specialty drinks. 2x/week = ~$40/mo.

monthlyPass.notes: HRT 31-day pass $60 regular, $30 reduced (seniors 65+, disabled, Medicare cardholders). The Tide light rail uses same fare system.

publicTransit.coverage: Hampton Roads Transit (HRT) operates 28 bus routes and The Tide light rail (7.4-mile route) in Norfolk. Service connects to Virginia Beach, Chesapeake, and across Hampton Roads via buses and Elizabeth River Ferry. Coverage is adequate for downtown/Ghent but limited in suburban areas.
publicTransit.seniorDiscount: Seniors 65+ and Medicare cardholders ride for $1.00 single ride or $30 monthly pass (50% discount). Reduced fare ID required.

rideShare.providers: Uber and Lyft both operate in Hampton Roads. Availability is good in downtown/Ghent, moderate in suburban neighborhoods.

parkingPerMonth.notes: Downtown parking $80-120/mo in garages. Most residential neighborhoods have free street parking. Ghent street parking is free but limited.

carOwnership.notes: A car is strongly recommended in Norfolk. While Ghent and downtown are walkable, most errands, medical appointments, and regional destinations require driving. Norfolk's flood-prone roads can cause detours during heavy rain. Virginia personal property tax applies to vehicles (assessed annually by city).

annualExamCost.notes: Routine eye exam $100-200 without insurance. With VSP or EyeMed, copay typically $10-25. Medicare covers eye exams only for specific conditions (diabetes, glaucoma risk).

vision.insuranceCoverage: VSP and EyeMed are the most common vision plans in Hampton Roads. Many Medicare Advantage plans include basic vision coverage. Costco Optical in Norfolk offers competitive pricing without insurance. Warby Parker has a store in Virginia Beach Town Center.
vision.specialistWaitTime: 1-3 weeks for routine ophthalmology appointments. Same-week availability for optometrists at retail locations (LensCrafters, Costco, Walmart Vision). Eastern Virginia Eye Associates and Virginia Eye Consultants are major practices.
vision.seniorConsiderations: Annual dilated eye exams recommended for seniors. Medicare covers diabetic eye exams and glaucoma screening for high-risk patients. Cataract surgery well-covered by Medicare at Sentara and local surgical centers.

annualCleaningCost.notes: Routine cleaning and exam $100-160 without insurance. With Delta Dental or similar, copay $0-30 for preventive.

commonProcedures.coverageNote: Rarely covered by insurance. Some plans offer $1000-1500 allowance.
Most plans cover 50% up to annual maximum

dental.insuranceCoverage: Delta Dental of Virginia is the largest dental insurer. Most employer and ACA plans include basic dental. Medicare does NOT cover routine dental — standalone dental plans ($20-50/mo) or Medicare Advantage with dental recommended. EVMS dental school clinic offers reduced-cost care.
dental.dentistAvailability: Good availability in Norfolk. Multiple dental practices in every neighborhood. Eastern Virginia Medical School has a dental residency program offering reduced-rate care. Wait times typically 1-2 weeks for routine appointments, same-day for emergencies.

plans.name: T-Mobile 55+ Essentials
plans.notes: 2 lines unlimited talk/text/data. VA wireless tax ~12% + federal USF. 5G included.

eyeExam.notes: Standard comprehensive eye exam. Most VSP/EyeMed plans cover 1 exam/year with $10-20 copay.

glasses.notes: Frames + single-vision lenses ~$100 with insurance allowance. Progressive lenses add $100-150. Contacts 12-month supply varies by brand.

insurance.coverage: VSP or EyeMed plan, 1 exam + $150 frame allowance/year
insurance.notes: Employer-style standalone vision plan. Covers 1 exam/year, $150 frame allowance, contacts allowance in lieu of frames.

cleaning.notes: Preventive cleaning 2x/year. Most plans cover 100% preventive.

filling.notes: Composite (tooth-colored) filling. Insurance typically covers 80% of basic procedures.

crown.notes: Porcelain-fused-to-metal crown. Insurance typically covers 50% of major procedures.

insurance.coverage: Delta Dental or equivalent, 2 cleanings/year, 80% basic, 50% major
insurance.notes: DHMO or PPO plan. Annual max $1,000-$1,500. Waiting periods may apply for major work.

items.name: Chicken (whole/thighs)
items.notes: Primary protein for both adults & dog

items.name: Ground Turkey / Beef
items.notes: Lean ground for dog food base

items.name: Organ Meat (liver/heart)
items.notes: Essential for balanced home-cooked dog diet

breakdown.notes: Water, sewage, and trash are separate utility bills — see utilities breakdown.

housing.taxNotes: US Mid-Atlantic does not impose rental tax. Property tax is the landlord's responsibility but factored into rent. Renter's insurance recommended but not legally required.
housing.leaseTerms: 12-month standard lease. Security deposit max 1-2 months rent ($2,025). Pet deposit common ($300-500).
housing.marketNotes: US Mid-Atlantic rental market. 2BR houses available through Zillow, Apartments.com, and local listings.

stores.name: Wegmans
stores.notes: Premium grocery with excellent prepared foods

stores.name: ShopRite
stores.notes: Co-op chain with strong weekly sales and couponing

stores.name: Giant
stores.notes: Regional chain with good produce selection

stores.name: Norfolk Farmers Market
stores.notes: Local farmers market, seasonal produce, often Saturday mornings.

healthcare.coverageSummary: Medicare Part A (free) + Part B ($174.70/mo pp) + Medigap Plan G (~$200-220/mo pp) + Part D (~$45/mo pp). IRA out-of-pocket cap $2,000/yr starting 2025.

costs.notes: US Mid-Atlantic age-rated premium. Higher due to AMD + multiple conditions.
Medicare covers 80%, Medigap covers 20% coinsurance. ~6-8 injections/yr.
OCT scans every 4-8 weeks. Medicare + Medigap covers most.
Quarterly bloodwork/visits. Low copay with Medigap.
Annual visit + as-needed. Mild asthma, infrequent.
US Mid-Atlantic age-rated premium. Diabetes management but no AMD.
All generics — lower tier plan sufficient
Quarterly A1C, bloodwork. Covered well by Medicare.
Medicare Part B covers glucose monitors. Some copays.

shared.notes: Separate dental plan ~$35/person. Medicare does not cover dental.
Basic vision plan. AMD specialist visits covered by Medicare Part B.
Covered 100% by Medicare — no copay for preventive.

blackHairCare.label: Black Hair Care
blackHairCare.notes: Norfolk area salons with experience in natural hair, locs, braids, and protective styles.

whiteHairCare.label: White/Caucasian Hair Care
whiteHairCare.notes: Standard salon and barbershop options throughout Norfolk.

mensBigTall.label: Men's Big & Tall Clothing
mensBigTall.notes: DXL is the primary brick-and-mortar option. Online retailers (Amazon, King Size) ship to Norfolk.

womensClothing.label: Women's Regular Clothing
womensClothing.notes: Range from budget (Target, Old Navy) to mid-range (LOFT, Banana Republic) available in Norfolk area.

shoes.label: Shoes
shoes.notes: DSW and shoe departments at major retailers available in Norfolk area. Wide width options at DSW and online.

gym.notes: Planet Fitness ($10-25/mo), LA Fitness, YMCA, and SilverSneakers (free with many Medicare Advantage plans) available in Norfolk area.

discounts.name: Medicare
discounts.description: Federal health insurance at 65. Part A (hospital) premium-free for most. Part B (medical) ~$175/month. Part D (drugs) $10-50/month.

discounts.name: AARP Membership
discounts.description: Annual membership $16. Discounts on insurance, dining, travel, retail, and entertainment. Available at age 50+.

discounts.name: US Mid-Atlantic Social Security Tax
discounts.description: Virginia fully exempts Social Security benefits from state income tax. $12,000 age deduction at 65+.

discounts.name: WMATA/VRE Senior Transit Fare
discounts.description: Reduced fare for riders 65+. Most transit systems offer half-fare or free rides for seniors with ID.

discounts.name: America the Beautiful Senior Pass
discounts.description: Lifetime pass to all national parks and federal recreation areas for $80 (or $20/year).

discounts.name: SilverSneakers Fitness
discounts.description: Free gym membership through participating Medicare Advantage and Medigap plans. Access to 17,000+ locations nationwide.
